Red Tails (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $49,876,377Details
International Box Office $489,121
Worldwide Box Office $50,365,498
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $16,824,944
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $6,581,265
Total $23,406,209
Further financial details...

Synopsis

1944. To help win the war, the Pentagon brass has no choice but to consider the untested African-American pilots of the experimental Tuskegee training program. Just as the young Tuskegee men are about to be shut down and shipped back home, they are given the ultimate chance to show their courage. These intrepid young airmen take to the skies to fight for their country - and the fate of the free world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,782,154 (37.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.66 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:99.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$58,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,512 opening theaters/2,573 max. theaters, 4.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $74,939,889

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: January 20th, 2012 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: June 6th, 2012 (Wide) (United Kingdom)
September 6th, 2012 (Wide) (Croatia)
Video Release: May 22nd, 2012 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some sequences of war violence.
(Rating bulletin 2197, 11/9/2011)
Running Time: 120 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Development Hell, Ensemble, Visual Effects
Plot point: African Americans, Bigotry, Discrimination, War
Release detail: Delayed Release

Subgenre: War Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: 20th Century Fox, Lucasfilm
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

1944. To help win the war, the Pentagon brass has no choice but to consider the untested African-American pilots of the experimental Tuskegee training program. Just as the young Tuskegee men are about to be shut down and shipped back home, they are given the ultimate chance to show their courage. These intrepid young airmen take to the skies to fight for their country - and the fate of the free world.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$18,782,154 (37.7% of total gross)
Legs:2.66 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:99.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$58,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,512 opening theaters/2,573 max. theaters, 4.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $74,939,889
